_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Ability to design security and privacy architectures that enable data utility and AI innovation while maintaining protection levels appropriate to the data sensitivity and regulatory context.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. Ability SP-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.455), SP-A-003 (SP, cosine: 0.447).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.618). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [awareness, create, cybersecurity, program] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, ai, appropriate, architectures, context, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.860 (P=0.888, R=0.834). P exceeds R by 0.053 — the NICE element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 26 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:12.0%, N:62.0%, C:26.0%). No dominant classification for "Create a cybersecurity awareness program" vs "Ability to design security and privacy architectures that enable data utility an" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.474 < STS 0.618 (gap -0.144). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [awareness, create, cybersecurity] and [ability, ai, appropriate] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [awareness, create, cybersecurity, program]. WIDAI terms: [ability, ai, appropriate, architectures, context, data].
